rulings.Case Title: CALEF HOUSE, LLC, A BUSINESS ENTITY VS. FAY SERVICING, LLC, A BUSINESS ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 28, 2020 line 4. OTHER HMC ASSETS, LLC, SOLELY AS ADMINISTRATOR FOR DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurrer to the cause of action for violation of 12 CFR 1024.40 is sustained without leave to amend. No substantive opposition filed. Demurer to the cause of action for Elder Abuse to the extent it is asserted by the LLC is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs cite no authority for the proposition that this cause of action may be brought by an LLC. Demurrer is otherwise sustained with leave to amend for each Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each cause of action against each defendant. Based on the allegations in the Complaint Plaintiff Calef has standing to bring causes of action asserted in the complaint as a guarantor. See DOT. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Further, while Plaintiff appeared to have agreed to pay certain amounts under the loan in bankruptcy, the nature of that agreement is unclear, i.e. was Plaintiff agreeing to the amount to resolve the matter through bankruptcy, was the agreement made due to reliance on the amounts demanded by the defendant, was the agreement applicable to any other types of resolution, e.g. payment outside of bankruptcy. These factual issues cannot be resolved on a demurrer. Furthermore, the Court notes that in providing their calculations regarding excessive amount demanded in NOTS, Plaintiff does not allege that the amount could not have been properly and legally increased due to charges other than missed monthly payments, e.g. late fees, attorney's fees etc. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE GISIE VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 28, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING, INC. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to delayed discovery. Plaintiff's causes of action arise solely out of purportedly fraudulent recordation of the Assignment on May 19, 2014. The statute of limitations for all causes of action rooted in this recordation started on that date, unless Plaintiff can in good faith allege delayed discovery. Plaintiff's theory that the triggering event for the statute of limitations is a review of the documents by counsel is not supported by any authority. Plaintiff shall allege in good faith facts showing that her discovery of the Assignment occurred within 3 years of September 24, 2019. =(501/CFH)
